question:
Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Bobby can deadlift 300 pounds at 13.  When he is 18 he can deadlift 100 pounds more than _______% of his previous deadlift.  How many pounds did he add per year?
response:
Bobby can deadlift 300 pounds at 13. When he is 18 he can deadlift 100 pounds more than **50%** of his previous deadlift. How many pounds did he add per year?

Okay, so I think 50% of 300 is 150. Then 100 pounds more than that would be 250 pounds. But that number doesn’t seem right because 250 is less than 300, so maybe I messed up. I’m not totally sure, but I think I should use 50% because 100 pounds is a lot. For the pounds per year, I think it’s 100 pounds divided by 5 years, so maybe 20 pounds per year? Not sure if I’m right.
